definition_short: Average number of drought events per year per pixel; a drought event = three consecutive months with < 50% of average precipitation. (Labelled "SPI" in the CRI doc; in the source it is a bespoke drought-event-frequency count, not the WMO-standard SPI.)
Probability of a failed growing season: sufficient rainfall at start for germination/establishment, fewer than 50 growing days, and a clearly defined end. Reported as % cropping reliability.
source_citation: Hyman et al. 2025 (Failed Season) [NOTE: doc's cited Hyman 2025 is a Gulf of Mexico fishery paper (arXiv 2503.17293) -- mis-citation; correct source TBC]
production_specific: Yes (based on cost of production exceeding harvest value; no specific crop)

definition_short: Renewable freshwater available to a sub-basin with upstream consumption removed (internal runoff + upstream discharge). cm/year.

indicator: Gross water demand and net consumption
definition_short: Maximum potential sectoral water demand and portion lost in use, for domestic/industrial/irrigation/livestock. 8 monthly gridded sets, Jan 1960-Dec 2019.
Ratio of total water demand to available renewable surface + groundwater supplies. Higher = more competition.
Global flood frequency from extreme flood events 1985-2003, georeferenced to nearest degree, classified into 10 classes.
Water volume in river flood plains, 1-in-10 annual probability event, RCP8.5; ISI-MIP forcing 2010-2049; bias-corrected rainfall. Metres above ground level.
Coastal flood risk incl. sea-level rise, no flood protection assumed; GTSR extreme water levels 1979-2014; MERIT DEM 30"; 1-in-10 event, metres above ground.
Areas where length of growing period flips from >120 days/yr to <120 days/yr. Growing day = mean air temp >6C and actual/potential ET ratio >0.35.
Areas where length of growing period flips from >90 days/yr to <90 days/yr (same LGP definition as R-01).
Areas where CV of annual rainfall (SD/mean, %) currently exceeds the global 75th percentile (28%).
Between-year variability of available water supply (surface + groundwater); 30-year periods centred on 2030/2050/2080.
Within-year variability of available water supply (surface + groundwater).
Annual count of days with max Wet Bulb Globe Temperature (WBGTmax) > 28/30/32 C (ISO occupational-heat thresholds). 0.05 deg, 1983-2016.

Areas where length of growing period flips from >120 days/yr to <120 days/yr. NOTE: wording is verbatim identical to R-01; appears to be a duplicate of the changed-rainfall LGP row placed under High Temperature.
Areas where mean daily max temperature flips from <30 C to >30 C during the primary growing season, masked where LGP > 40 days.
Spatial distribution of salt-affected soils: ECe > 2 dS/m and/or ESP > 15% and/or pH > 8.2.
source_citation: FAO Global Soil Partnership GSASmap (FAO n.d.; Omuto et al. 2020)

definition_short: Soil salinity as ECe index classes: slightly (2-4), moderately (4-8), highly (8-16), extremely (>16) saline.
source_citation: ISRIC Global Soil Salinity (ISRIC 2019; Ivushkin et al. 2019)

definition_short: Soil capacity to hold water/air at 1500 kPa, 30-60 cm depth. From WoSIS profiles + random-forest pedotransfer function.
Fraction of water soil can store and release for plant use = field capacity minus permanent wilting point, 30-60 cm.
Modelled root-zone storage from surface to weathered bedrock; sized to expected max cumulative water deficit within a return period.
Stored water available for plant use = field capacity minus wilting point in root zone; soil water retention model + Ksat + rainfall-gap logic over 37 years.
Spatial data on area actively irrigated and areas equipped but not actively cultivated; from national/subnational statistics 2000-2015, 243 countries.
source_citation: Mehta et al. 2024 (Area Equipped with Irrigation)
